questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 1

parts.prompt: List FIVE steps in the progressive management approach to an underperforming employee.
answer_key: Meet with the employee and/or supervisor to discuss the situation and gather facts.
Clearly communicate performance expectations to the employee.
Counsel the employee about performance gaps and ensure understanding of requirements.
Identify any learning or coaching needs or personal issues contributing to poor performance.
Address identified issues (e.g., training, employee assistance program).
Set specific performance improvement objectives with specific time frames.
Meet regularly with the employee/supervisor to monitor performance.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning

parts.prompt: List FOUR steps in the progressive discipline of an underperforming employee.
answer_key: Provide a verbal warning regarding unresolved poor performance.
Provide a written warning regarding continued poor performance.
Suspend the employee without pay for an escalating number of days.
End employment if performance does not improve.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning

questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 2
questions.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection

parts.prompt: List THREE questions for a hepatitis A infected food handler that impact immunoprophylaxis decisions.
answer_key: Was the food handler infectious while working?
Does the food handler handle foods that are not cooked or handle food after it is cooked?
Does the food handler have poor hygiene?
Did the food handler have diarrhoea while working?
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection

questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 3
questions.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection

parts.prompt: List FOUR factors that may affect the quality of well water.
answer_key: Depth of well (dug vs drilled).
Amount of new water flowing into the area.
Local land use activities (e.g., livestock, pesticides, gasoline storage).
Meteorological factors (e.g., heavy rains).
Natural quality of surrounding soil (e.g., elevated heavy metals).
Surface water infiltration.
Well and local hydrogeology (integrity of bedrock).
Quality of well-head construction (grouting/casing).
Location/distance from sources of contamination (e.g., septic tank).
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection

parts.prompt: If fecal coliforms are detected in well water, list TWO immediate recommendations you would give to the homeowner.
must_have_elements: Stop using untreated well water for consumption
answer_key: Stop using untreated well water for consumption.
Boil or disinfect the water before consuming.
Investigate and correct well condition (e.g., disinfect with bleach and resample).
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection

questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 4

parts.prompt: List FOUR benefits of breastfeeding for the child's health.
answer_key: Reduced rate of infections (otitis media, respiratory, gastrointestinal).
Reduced incidence of atopic diseases.
Prevention of certain chronic diseases (e.g., diabetes, obesity, Crohn’s disease).
Prevention of cognitive development abnormalities.
Enhanced mother-child bonding.
Transfer of immunoglobulins from mother to child.
Decreased risk of childhood leukemia, SIDS and necrotising enterocolitis.
Provides optimal nutrition.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Promotion and Disease Prevention

parts.prompt: List TWO process indicators and TWO outcome indicators to evaluate a breastfeeding promotion and support program.
answer_key: Process: number or proportion of staff trained to promote/support breastfeeding.
Process: number or proportion of program participants.
Process: duration of participation in the program.
Process: number of service organisations favourable to breastfeeding (e.g., baby-friendly hospitals).
Outcome: participants' knowledge/attitudes regarding benefits of breastfeeding.
Outcome: staff knowledge/attitudes regarding importance of breastfeeding.
Outcome: uptake of breastfeeding among participants (number/proportion).
Outcome: duration of breastfeeding among participants (average, median).
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning

questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 5
questions.official_written_classification_label: Basic Sciences of Public Health and Preventive Medicine

parts.prompt: List TWO types of biases that can cause the healthy worker effect in a cohort study design.
answer_key: Healthy hire effect: selection bias at hiring resulting in entry of healthier workers into the workforce.
Healthy worker survivor effect: healthier workers are more likely to remain employed.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Basic Sciences of Public Health and Preventive Medicine

questions.title: Royal College Official Sample SAQ Question 6

parts.prompt: List the PRIMARY health concern directly associated with excessive dietary sodium intake.
answer_key: Hypertension.
not_acceptable_notes: Other cardiovascular conditions are not acceptable.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Promotion and Disease Prevention

parts.prompt: List THREE steps of a structured voluntary approach by industry to reach sodium reduction targets in processed foods.
answer_key: Publication/establishment of sodium reduction targets for foods.
Defined timelines for implementation.
Mechanism for public commitment by industry to the targets.
Plan for monitoring progress by an external body.
Plan for independent evaluation of success with option for stronger regulatory action.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Health Systems

parts.prompt: List THREE reasons why the food industry is slow to reduce the salt content in processed foods.
answer_key: Sodium is a preservative important for food safety and hard to replicate.
Sodium is inexpensive and reformulation is costly.
Consumers prefer salty foods.
Sodium reduction is voluntary; early adopters may lose competitive advantage.
Sodium increases thirst, boosting beverage sales.
parts.scoring_notes: Award credit for distinct correct elements up to max_score; preserve official part-score cap.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Health Systems

questions.title: HR, Equity, and Power Differentials – Performance Concern with Possible Manager Bias
questions.stem: An employee is labelled ‘underperforming’ by their manager at the local health unit. The employee reports being late due to religious observances and feels targeted. You note a power differential and potential conflict of interest.

parts.prompt: List SIX steps you would take to assess the situation fairly and safely.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 3 marks total Any SIX: - Clarify concerns with objective expectations and specific examples - Gather evidence from multiple sources (documentation, metrics) - Meet separately with employee and manager; use equity‑informed approach - Consult HR early; screen for discrimination/harassment - Consider accommodation obligations and explore reasonable accommodations - Document and maintain confidentiality - Consider interim measures to reduce harm (e.g., temporary reporting change)
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List THREE indicators that the manager may be the primary problem rather than the employee.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 1.5 marks total Any THREE: - Inconsistent standards applied across staff - Lack of documented coaching/support prior to escalation - Reports/patterns of biased language, harassment, or retaliation - High turnover/complaints in the manager’s team
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: Propose TWO actions to address governance/line-of-sight and reduce conflict of interest during the investigation.
answer_key: Assign an independent investigator/decision-maker outside the direct line relationship.
Establish clear reporting and oversight (e.g., HR/legal governance) to avoid conflict and ensure fairness.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

questions.title: Extreme Heat and Air Quality – Vulnerability, Indices, Syndemics

parts.prompt: List FIVE vulnerable populations at higher risk of heat‑related illness.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 2.5 marks total Any FIVE: - Older adults - Infants and young children - People with chronic medical conditions - People taking medications affecting thermoregulation/hydration - Socially isolated or mobility‑limited individuals - People experiencing homelessness or inadequate cooling - Outdoor workers/athletes
parts.official_written_classification_label: Population Health
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: Differentiate between the Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) and an Air Quality Index (AQI).
answer_key: AQHI communicates short-term health risk and behaviour guidance; AQI is primarily pollutant concentration/status index and may not map directly to individual health action.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Population Health
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

parts.prompt: Define a syndemic and give one example relevant to co‑occurring extreme heat and poor air quality.
answer_key: 3 marks total - Definition (1): synergistic interaction of two or more health problems under conditions of inequity that worsens outcomes. - Example (1): extreme heat + wildfire smoke worsening COPD/asthma admissions, amplified by precarious housing. - Public health implication (1): integrated messaging and targeted supports (cooling/clean‑air shelters, outreach).
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

questions.title: Measles – Case Management and Post‑Exposure Prophylaxis
questions.stem: A child presents to Emergency with fever, cough, conjunctivitis and a generalized maculopapular rash; measles is suspected.

parts.prompt: List THREE immediate infection control/public health steps.
answer_key: 1 mark each, 3 marks total Any THREE: - Airborne isolation and notify infection prevention and control - Urgent public health notification and case investigation - Collect appropriate specimens and arrange testing
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: As you consider exposed cases for post-exposure prophylaxis eligibility, list TWO options and the time window for each.
answer_key: MMR vaccine within 72 hours of exposure for eligible susceptible contacts.
Immune globulin within 6 days of exposure for high-risk susceptible contacts.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

parts.prompt: List THREE groups prioritized for Ig.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 1.5 marks total Any THREE: - Pregnant people without evidence of immunity - Immunocompromised individuals - Infants <6 months - Immunocompetent infants 6–11 months who present after 72 hours and within 6 days
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List TWO criteria for evidence of measles immunity.
answer_key: 1 mark each, 2 marks total Any TWO: - Documentation of 2 doses of measles‑containing vaccine (age‑appropriate) - Laboratory evidence of immunity - History of laboratory‑confirmed measles
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

questions.title: Tuberculosis – Newcomer in Congregate Housing
questions.stem: A newcomer to Canada living in a dorm-like setting is diagnosed with pulmonary TB. They are worried about stigma and unsure about taking oral therapy.

parts.prompt: List FOUR factors that make a TB case higher risk for transmission or poor outcomes.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 2 marks total Any FOUR: - Smear‑positive pulmonary TB - Cavitary disease on imaging - Significant cough/high symptom burden - Delayed diagnosis/prolonged infectious period - Congregate living setting - HIV/immunocompromise
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List FOUR strategies to support rapport and culturally safe care.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 2 marks total Any FOUR: - Use professional interpreters; avoid family as interpreter - Trauma‑informed and non‑stigmatizing communication - Explain confidentiality and what must be shared for public health - Address practical barriers (transportation, housing, food, clinic access)
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List THREE approaches to improve oral medication adherence.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 1.5 marks total Any THREE: - DOT or video‑enabled DOT (VDOT) - Case management, reminders, incentives/enablers - Education on duration, side effects, and missed dose plan
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: As you communicate potential TB exposure with other tenants in the dorm, list TWO principles to balance confidentiality with exposure management.
answer_key: Share only minimum necessary information and protect personal identifiers.
Provide clear exposure/risk guidance and access to testing/follow-up while avoiding stigmatizing language.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override (HP=Health Protection, not HPDP).

parts.prompt: List SIX infections in the prenatal/newborn period that are high public health priority because they can cause severe outcomes and/or have clear prevention opportunities.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 3 marks total Any SIX: - Hepatitis B - HIV - Syphilis - Rubella (congenital rubella syndrome prevention) - Varicella (congenital/neonatal varicella prevention) - Measles (high consequence; PEP and outbreak management) - Pertussis (severe infant disease; prevention via maternal Tdap) - Influenza (severe disease in infants; maternal vaccination + infant vaccination starting at 6 months)
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List TWO vaccines that can begin at 6 months of age (seasonal/routine recommendations) AND list FOUR vaccines commonly given around 12 months of age in many Canadian schedules.
answer_key: 6 months: influenza, COVID-19 (per recommendations).
Around 12 months: MMR, Men-C-C, PCV booster, varicella/MMRV (schedule dependent).
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

questions.title: Lead Exposure – Sources and Community Hazard Assessment
questions.stem: A child is found to have elevated blood lead levels.

parts.prompt: List SIX possible sources of lead exposure.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 3 marks total Any SIX: - Lead‑based paint and household dust in older housing - Soil contamination - Drinking water from lead service lines/plumbing/solder - Imported toys/jewelry/cosmetics/spices/traditional remedies containing lead - Take‑home exposure from parental occupations/hobbies - Lead‑glazed pottery used for food
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: The suspected source is a water fountain at a local daycare. List FOUR steps in a hazard/risk assessment for other potentially exposed children.
answer_key: Define exposed population and exposure period.
Obtain environmental sampling/testing data to characterize hazard.
Assess dose/exposure pathways and susceptibility (age, pregnancy, etc.).
Plan clinical/public health follow-up and communication based on risk stratification.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

questions.title: Emergency Management – Respiratory Virus Season Cycle
questions.stem: Your public health unit plans for an annual respiratory virus season (COVID-19, RSV, influenza) peaking in late winter with an annual emergency management cycle.

parts.prompt: List the FOUR phases of emergency management.
answer_key: 2 marks total - Mitigation/Prevention (0.5) - Preparedness (0.5) - Response (1) - Recovery (1)
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: Apply each phase to a respiratory virus season cycle by listing ONE concrete activity under each phase.
answer_key: Mitigation activity.
Preparedness activity.
Response activity.
Recovery activity.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

parts.prompt: In the recovery phase (‘hot wash’) after the peak, list FOUR items that should be reviewed.
answer_key: What worked well and key enablers.
Gaps/failures and root causes.
Data/timeliness/communications performance.
Resource/staffing/surge implications and improvement actions before next season.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Management and Program Planning
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

questions.title: iGAS Cluster – Homeless Shelter
questions.stem: A homeless shelter reports multiple severe invasive Group A Streptococcus (iGAS) cases.

parts.prompt: List THREE clinical severity features suggesting streptococcal toxic shock syndrome (STSS).
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 1.5 marks total Any THREE: - Hypotension/shock - Multi‑organ dysfunction (renal impairment, coagulopathy, liver involvement) - Soft tissue necrosis / severe pain out of proportion / rapidly progressive infection
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List FOUR factors that define a higher‑risk contact situation in a shelter context.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 2 marks total Any FOUR: - Close contact/shared sleeping arrangements - Open wounds/skin breakdown - Injection drug use - Recent influenza/varicella‑like illness - Immunocompromise
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: List TWO considerations for whether to offer chemoprophylaxis and one possible regimen class.
answer_key: Intensity/proximity of exposure and contact risk profile.
Feasibility, adherence, resistance, and harms/benefits in setting.
Possible regimen class: beta-lactam or macrolide (per guidance/susceptibility).
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

questions.title: Suspected Bat Exposure – Rabies Risk Assessment and PEP
questions.stem: A child wakes up and a bat is found in the bedroom. No obvious bite marks are seen on the child.

parts.prompt: List THREE exposure history elements that determine whether rabies PEP is indicated.
answer_key: 0.5 mark each, 1.5 marks total Any THREE: - Evidence of direct contact with bat (bat touching skin; child touching bat) or inability to reliably exclude contact - Bat available for testing - Presence of bite/scratch or mucous membrane exposure to saliva
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Both legacy rc_classification and theme are present at part level and were carried forward exactly.

parts.prompt: If PEP is indicated and the child is not previously vaccinated, list FOUR components.
answer_key: Immediate wound cleansing/irrigation.
Human rabies immune globulin dosing and infiltration.
Rabies vaccine day 0.
Complete follow-up vaccine doses per schedule.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.

parts.prompt: If the bat is captured and tests negative, what is the implication for PEP?
answer_key: PEP can be withheld or discontinued if reliable testing confirms bat is rabies negative.
parts.official_written_classification_label: Intervention and Methods in Health Protection
parts.taxonomy_assignment_rationale: Approved manual adjudication override.
